title: Console chat maintains conversational context across long sessions

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
**As a** Plexus console user  
**I want** the assistant to remember context from earlier in long conversations  
**So that** I can say "do that again" or reference prior actions without repeating details

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: Console chat conversational continuity
  The console assistant must maintain context across long conversations
  so users can reference prior actions without repeating parameters.

  Scenario: User asks to repeat a prior action after 20+ turns
    Given I have a console chat session
    And I have had 20+ conversational turns with the assistant
    And I previously asked "Run evaluation on items A, B, C with score X"
    And the assistant completed that evaluation
    When I send the message "Do that again with items D, E, F"
    Then the assistant recognizes "that" refers to the prior evaluation
    And the assistant runs evaluation on items D, E, F with score X
    And the assistant does not ask me to clarify what "that" means

  Scenario: Context preserved across tool-heavy exchanges
    Given I have a console chat session
    And I execute several commands that generate large tool responses
    And the total conversation exceeds 50 messages
    When I reference a decision made 30 messages ago
    Then the assistant recalls the decision correctly
    And provides a response consistent with that prior context

  Scenario: Recent context takes precedence in compacted history
    Given I have a console chat session with 60+ messages
    And message history has been compacted with rolling summary
    When I reference something from the recent 10 messages
    Then the assistant recalls it with full detail
    And does not rely solely on the summary
```
